content:
# Judges Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_270.jpg, JDG_page_271.jpg

1. And the sons of Yisrael again did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — and Ehud had died.

2. And YHWH sold them into the hand of Yavin king of Canaan, who reigned in Chatsor. And the commander of his army [was] Sisera, and he was dwelling in Charosheth-ha-Goyim.

3. And the sons of Yisrael cried out to YHWH, for nine hundred chariots of iron he had, and he oppressed the sons of Yisrael with force twenty years.

4. And Devorah, a woman, a prophetess, wife of Lappidoth — she was judging Yisrael at that time.

5. And she was sitting under the palm of Devorah, between Ramah and Beit-El, in the hill-country of Ephrayim. And the sons of Yisrael went up to her for judgment.

6. And she sent and called Baraq son of Avinoam from Kedesh-Naphtali, and she said to him, "Has not YHWH, God of Yisrael, commanded: 'Go and draw [forces] to Mount Tavor, and take with you ten thousand men from the sons of Naphtali and from the sons of Zevulun?

7. And I will draw to you, to the Wadi Qishon, Sisera, the commander of the army of Yavin, and his chariots and his multitude, and I will give him into your hand.'"

8. And Baraq said to her, "If you go with me, I will go; and if you do not go with me, I will not go."

9. And she said, "Going I will go with you; however, the glory will not be yours on the way which you go, for into the hand of a woman YHWH will sell Sisera." And Devorah arose and went with Baraq to Kedesh.

10. And Baraq summoned Zevulun and Naphtali to Kedesh, and ten thousand men went up at his feet. And Devorah went up with him.

11. And Chever the Kenite had separated from the Kenites, from the sons of Chovav, the father-in-law of Moshe, and he pitched his tent as far as the oak in Tsaanannim, which [is] near Kedesh.

12. And they told Sisera that Baraq son of Avinoam had gone up to Mount Tavor.

13. And Sisera summoned all his chariots — nine hundred chariots of iron — and all the people who [were] with him, from Charosheth-ha-Goyim to the Wadi Qishon.

14. And Devorah said to Baraq, "Arise, for this [is] the day in which YHWH has given Sisera into your hand. Has not YHWH gone out before you?" And Baraq went down from Mount Tavor, and ten thousand men after him.

15. And YHWH routed Sisera and all the chariots and all the camp with the edge of the sword before Baraq. And Sisera dismounted from the chariot and fled on his feet.

16. And Baraq pursued after the chariots and after the camp as far as Charosheth-ha-Goyim. And all the camp of Sisera fell by the edge of the sword; not even one remained.

17. And Sisera fled on his feet to the tent of Yael, wife of Chever the Kenite, for [there was] peace between Yavin king of Chatsor and the house of Chever the Kenite.

18. And Yael went out to meet Sisera, and she said to him, "Turn aside, my lord, turn aside to me. Do not fear." And he turned aside to her, to the tent, and she covered him with a rug.

19. And he said to her, "Please give me a little water to drink, for I am thirsty." And she opened the skin of milk and gave him drink and covered him.

20. And he said to her, "Stand at the entrance of the tent, and it will be, if a man comes and asks you and says, 'Is there a man here?' — you shall say, 'There is not.'"

21. And Yael, wife of Chever, took the tent-peg and put the hammer in her hand, and she went to him softly, and she drove the peg into his temple, and it went down into the ground — and he was in a deep sleep and exhausted — and he died.

22. And behold, Baraq [was] pursuing Sisera. And Yael went out to meet him and said to him, "Come, and I will show you the man whom you are seeking." And he came to her, and behold, Sisera [was] fallen, dead, and the peg [was] in his temple.

23. And God subdued on that day Yavin king of Canaan before the sons of Yisrael.

24. And the hand of the sons of Yisrael went, going and hard, upon Yavin king of Canaan, until they had cut off Yavin king of Canaan.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "A woman, a prophetess":** ishah n'vi'ah — the Hebrew stresses both her gender and her office. She is the only judge explicitly called a prophet/prophetess.

2. **v.9 — "Into the hand of a woman":** Deborah's prophecy is deliberately ambiguous — the reader expects the "woman" to be Deborah, but it turns out to be Jael.

3. **v.11 — Heber the Kenite:** This parenthetical verse, seemingly out of place, is narratively essential: it explains why Sisera would flee to the tent of a Kenite woman.

4. **v.21 — "She went to him softly":** vattavo elav ballat — the adverb ballat ("softly/secretly") heightens the tension. The act is described with deliberate detail: tent-peg, hammer, temple, ground.

— EMET, AI Translator
